regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an acute convergence of military devastation, internal power fragmentation, and diplomatic contradictions as the new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issues defiant threats while covert negotiations proceed through multiple channels with competing Iranian factions and a Trump April 6 deadline for infrastructure strikes if Strait of Hormuz demands aren't met. The regime simultaneously experiences economic collapse, elevated executions of political prisoners, hardline IRGC dominance over civilian leadership, and cascading military losses as US B-52 campaigns target underground infrastructure and Iran announces new offensive waves despite having lost two-thirds of its missile arsenal.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump announced Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday and presented a 15-point peace plan covering Strait reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback, while simultaneously threatening simultaneous strikes on all electric plants and oil facilities by April 6 if no deal is reached; Iran's Foreign Minister confirmed reviewing the plan without intent to negotiate while parliament speaker denied talks were occurring, creating fundamental ambiguity about regime decision-making authority.
